repo_prior_art.cite: none; doc/API.md section 3 has only Manski-style PartialID entries and no Lee-bound denominator-coupled Frechet-selector program

published_prior_art.cite: LeeLiu2025ContinuousLee Remark 3 explicitly says the conditional sufficient-set sharp bound is out of scope and gives an implementable non-sharp bound; Dinkelbach1967FractionalProgramming supplies only generic fractional-program machinery

repo_prior_art.cite: none; no in-catalogue qid states a common-threshold collapse certificate for pointwise Frechet plug-in sharpness

published_prior_art.cite: JonzonGabrielSjolanderSachs2025CovariatesBounds separates pointwise from uniform sharpness but does not derive this Lee-Liu sufficient-set threshold condition
LeeLiu2025ContinuousLee identifies the sufficient-value case with pi_AT(X)=min_d s(d,X) and the corresponding trimmed functional

ColangeloLee2025ContinuousDML gives kernel-localized DML for point-identified ADRFs and Semenova2024GeneralizedLee gives Lee-bound orthogonal moment templates, but neither covers this denominator-coupled selector root
FangSantos2019DirectionalInference gives generic directional-delta/resampling theory, not this Lee-type Dinkelbach endpoint map

soundness_flags.label: prop:regular-dr-endpoint / def:regular-root-objects
soundness_flags.one_line: The kernel-localized signal is treated as having exact conditional mean N_sigma and the limit variance is written as Var[varphi_sigma], but with a 1/h_n kernel factor the statement needs an explicit kernel-bias residual, local-dose smoothness, and an h_n-scaled limiting variance.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: prop:regular-dr-endpoint / ass:interior-curvature
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The regular inference result is conditional on a novel smooth-root interior-curvature submodel and does not claim coverage at selector ties or Frechet-boundary optima.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Gaussian Wald inference is assessed only on laws satisfying ass:interior-curvature; boundary-uniform coverage remains the OEQ.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: prop:regular-dr-endpoint / ass:band-estimability and ass:trimmed-numerator-estimability
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The endpoint CLT is conditional on high-level L2 estimability of the Frechet-band endpoints and trimmed numerator process; those nuisance rates are not claimed to follow from Lee-Liu or Semenova.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The regular endpoint expansion is a conditional high-level nuisance-rate claim, not a theorem deriving first-stage rates from primitive smoothness classes.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Repair prop:regular-dr-endpoint by separating the finite-bandwidth kernel score from its h_n -> 0 limit: add local smoothness in d, an explicit O(h_n^2) bias term killed by undersmoothing, and a limiting variance such as lim h_n Var[varphi_{sigma,n}].
Add a two- or three-covariate finite-X exhibit computing H_sigma, the Dinkelbach root, and a strict gap from the pointwise Frechet plug-in when C_sigma fails.
Anchor the LeeLiu2025ContinuousLee gap to Remark 3 in Section 6, where the paper says the conditional sufficient-set sharp bound is out of scope and gives a likely non-sharp bound.
State a boundary corollary for the sufficient-value reduction showing that the Dinkelbach root equals the known Lee-Liu ratio when underline_pi_d=overline_pi_d.

recommended_next_step: Continue revise on this angle: the field-level novelty survives, but the regular endpoint inference statement needs kernel-bias and variance normalization repairs before Stage 0.
